title: React Development on Android — Complete Guide | Acode
description: Build React applications on your Android phone with Acode. Full React development environment with Node.js, npm, terminal, and live preview. Create React apps anywhere, anytime.
heroHeadline: Build React Apps on Your Android Phone
heroSubheadline: Acode gives you a complete React development environment on Android. Create React App, Vite, JSX syntax highlighting, live preview, Node.js, and npm — all running natively on your phone.
problemHeadline: React Development Needs a Desktop — Right?
problemPoints: React development requires Node.js, npm, a terminal, and a browser for preview. These are desktop tools. Running them on Android has been considered impossible — until now.
Sure, you can edit JSX files in a text editor app, but you can't run them. You can't npm install. You can't start a dev server. You can't see your components render. You're editing blind.
React developers who want to code on the go have had no real option. They either carry a laptop everywhere or wait until they're at their desk. Mobile development has been a dream, not reality.
solutionPoints: Acode's Alpine Linux terminal runs Node.js and npm natively. Run npx create-react-app my-app or npm create vite@latest to scaffold a React project. It works exactly like it does on desktop.
JSX/TSX syntax highlighting is built into Acode's editor. Your React components are color-coded and readable. Install the React Snippets plugin for component templates and shortcuts.
Start the dev server with npm start or npm run dev. Your React app runs locally on your phone. Preview it in Acode's built-in browser or any web browser on your device.
The complete React workflow on Android: write components in the editor, see errors in the terminal, debug in the JavaScript console, commit with Git, and deploy — all from your phone.
setupHeadline: Create Your First React App on Acode

setupSteps.title: Install Acode & Terminal
setupSteps.description: Download Acode and set up the Alpine Linux terminal (first launch only).

setupSteps.title: Install Node.js
setupSteps.description: Run: apk update && apk add nodejs npm

setupSteps.title: Create a React Project
setupSteps.description:
Choose your setup:
npx create-react-app my-app (CRA)
or npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react (Vite — faster).

setupSteps.title: Start Development
setupSteps.description: Run: cd my-app && npm start (CRA) or npm run dev (Vite). Your React app is now live on your phone.

setupSteps.title: Edit Components
setupSteps.description: Open src/App.js in Acode's editor. Make changes. See them update instantly with hot module replacement.

setupSteps.title: Install Additional Dependencies
setupSteps.description: Need React Router? npm install react-router-dom. Tailwind? npm install tailwindcss. Any npm package works.

features.title: JSX/TSX Support
features.description: Full syntax highlighting for JSX and TSX. Acode's editor understands React's component syntax and provides clear visual distinction between markup and logic.

features.title: Hot Module Replacement
features.description: Changes you make to React components are reflected instantly in the browser. Vite's HMR works perfectly through Acode's terminal and browser.

features.title: React Developer Tools
features.description: Debug your React apps using the JavaScript console, terminal output, and browser DevTools. Full debugging capabilities on mobile.

features.title: Component Libraries
features.description: Install and use Material UI, Ant Design, Chakra UI, or any React component library via npm. They all work on Acode.

features.title: State Management
features.description: Redux, Zustand, Jotai, React Context — all work on Acode. Install via npm and use in your components.

features.title: Tailwind CSS
features.description: Install Tailwind CSS plugin for Acode to get IntelliSense for Tailwind classes. Edit utility classes with autocompletion.

faqs.question: Can I really build a full React app on Android?
faqs.answer: Yes. Acode provides everything you need: Node.js, npm, terminal, editor with JSX support, and live preview. You can scaffold, develop, and test a complete React application on your phone.

faqs.question: Does Create React App work on Acode?
faqs.answer: Yes, but Vite is recommended for faster startup and better mobile performance. Both work — Vite is significantly faster on mobile devices.

faqs.question: Can I run React Native on Acode?
faqs.answer: React Native development is limited on Android since it requires native build tools. For React Native, use a desktop. For React web apps, Acode is perfect.

faqs.question: Can I deploy my React app from Acode?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use npm run build to create a production build. Then deploy via Git (push to GitHub and use Vercel/Netlify) or FTP (upload build files to your server).
